articles.title: Wall Street ends lower on semiconductor selloff as AI spending concerns mount
articles.summary: S&P 500 -1.44% to 7,365.47, Nasdaq -2.21% to 25,587.04, Dow -0.09% to 51,665.49. SOX -7.9%. MU -13%, SNDK -13%, AMD -9.4%, NVDA -4.1%. VIX +2.23 to 19.52. Six of 11 S&P sectors positive — consumer staples +1.8%. Volume 24.1B vs 22.53B avg.
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500: -1.44% to 7,365.47. Nasdaq: -2.21% to 25,587.04. Dow: -0.09% to 51,665.49. SOX: -7.9%. VIX: 19.52 (+2.23). Micron: -13%. SanDisk: -13%. AMD: -9.4%. Marvell: -5.8%. Nvidia: -4.1%. Alphabet: -1%. S&P Info Tech: -3.7%. Consumer Staples: +1.8%. Advancers/Decliners NYSE: 1.31-to-1 declining. Volume: 24.1B shares. Traders pricing second rate hike by December. 'Some of the news lately about AI raises questions about all the spending that's being done' — Thomas Martin, Globalt. Micron earnings Wednesday, PCE Thursday.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Stocks Slide as Tech Sell-Off Expands to Memory, Chips; SpaceX Bounces Off Record Low
articles.summary: Nasdaq -1.7%, S&P -1.1%, Dow +0.2%. Memory ETF (Roundhill) -12%. KOSPI -10% triggered two circuit breakers. SNDK -14%, WDC -9%, MU -12%. TSLA -6%. MSFT +2%. SpaceX +4% to ~$161 from $147 low. AMC -25% on $200M stock sale. Cerebras earnings after close: ±13% implied move. AVGO at $380, down 21% from $481 peak.
articles.extracted_text: Memory/chip rout: SNDK -14%, WDC -9%, MU -12%. KOSPI -10% (two trading halts). Samsung -12%, SK Hynix -13%. GOOGL extended Monday losses. NVDA -3%. TSLA -6%. MSFT +2%. SpaceX bounced +4% to ~$161 from intraday low $147 (lowest since IPO). AMC -25% to $2.08 on $200M stock sale. QCOM -8.5% amid $4B Modular Inc acquisition report. Cerebras earnings: consensus -$0.16/sh loss, rev $183.26M (+80% YoY), ±13% implied options move. AVGO at $380, down 21% from $481 peak despite strong Q2. Fed Chair Warsh adopting Greenspan-like communication, cutting FOMC statement word count by ~half, removing forward guidance.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 fall as BoFA rate hike note, Asian market tumble sends semis sinking
articles.summary: Three drivers: (1) BoFA note floating up to 3 rate hikes in 2026, no cuts until 2028; 2Y yield at highest since Feb 2025. (2) KOSPI -9.99% crash, circuit breakers triggered, Samsung -12%, SK Hynix -13%. (3) Iran rejecting IAEA inspections, claiming Strait of Hormuz control. SMH ETF -6.5%. Nearly 60% of S&P stocks green but tech dragged indices. Defensive outperformers: PSA +4.4%, IBM +4.2%, ACN +3.3%.
articles.extracted_text: Bank of America: up to three 25bp hikes in 2026, no cuts until 2028. 2Y yield highest since Feb 2025. 10Y at 4.495%. KOSPI -9.99% (largest drop in 3+ months, two 20-min trading halts). Samsung -12%, SK Hynix -13%. 'Clearly this will cause selling pressure and white knuckles for tech stocks' — Wedbush analyst Dan Ives. Iran: Strait of Hormuz 'will never return to pre-war conditions.' WTI $73.79 (-0.09%), Brent $77.80 (-0.13%). Gold $4,147.50 (-1.31%), Silver $63.09 (-4.50%). SMH -6.5% to $625.62. ORCL disclosed 21K job cuts (13% of workforce), restructuring costs $1.8B. AMC -25% on $200M offering. SPCX bounced ~+4% from $147 intraday low.

articles.title: How major US stock indexes fared Tuesday 6/23/2026
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500 fell 107.33 points, or 1.4%, to 7,365.46. Dow fell 45.87 points, or 0.1%, to 51,666.84. Nasdaq fell 579.56 points, or 2.2%, to 25,587.04. Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points, or 1%, to 2,975.48. For the week: S&P -1.8%, Dow +0.2%, Nasdaq -3.5%, Russell -0.1%. For the year: S&P +7.6%, Dow +7.5%, Nasdaq +10.1%, Russell +19.9%.

articles.title: Fed Rate Hikes Are Coming, Bank Of America Warns In Shocking Hawkish Call
articles.summary: BoFA forecasts 75bp of rate hikes by end-2026: three consecutive 25bp hikes in Sep, Oct, Dec. Fed funds target: 4.25-4.50%. Core PCE may hit 3.5% in May (~70bp above year-ago). 'Inflation has gotten unambiguously worse.' — Aditya Bhave, BoFA economist. Shift from 'risk management to supply shock management.' Q2 GDP tracking 2.8% annualized. Market pricing only 2 hikes by March 2027.
articles.extracted_text: Bank of America calls for 75bp of rate hikes by end-2026: three consecutive 25bp hikes (September, October, December). Federal funds rate target: 4.25-4.50%. Core PCE inflation may hit 3.5% in May (~70bp above year-ago levels). 'Inflation has gotten unambiguously worse.' — Aditya Bhave. Labor market 'firmed up this year' removing justification for 2025's 75bp of easing. Q2 GDP tracking 2.8% annualized. Shift from 'risk management to supply shock management.' Market pricing only two hikes by March 2027 — BofA is 25bp more and three months earlier.

articles.title: Oracle workforce shrinks by about 21,000 employees amid AI adoption
articles.summary: ORCL: 21,000 job cuts (13% of workforce) in fiscal 2026. Headcount: 141,000 as of May 31, 2026 vs 162,000 year prior. Restructuring costs soared to $1.84B from $374M. Net capex outlook: ~$70B fiscal 2027. Plans to raise $40B in debt and equity. Signed massive data-center deals with OpenAI and Meta. Stock down ~10% YTD.
articles.extracted_text: Oracle cut 21,000 jobs (13% of workforce) in fiscal 2026. Headcount: 141,000 as of May 31, 2026 vs ~162,000 FY2025. Restructuring costs: $1.84B (vs $374M prior year). First time below 150,000 in four years. Factors: management changes, product changes, performance, strategic shifts, acquisitions. Net capex outlook: ~$70B. Plans to raise $40B in debt/equity including $20B stock issuance. Signed data-center deals with OpenAI and Meta. Stock -10% YTD. 196 tech companies laid off 119,800+ employees YTD per Layoffs.fyi.

articles.title: Qualcomm nears $4B deal for AI chip startup Modular Inc
articles.summary: QCOM in advanced talks to acquire Modular Inc for ~$4B. Follows earlier $8-10B Tenstorrent pursuit — potential $14B+ AI silicon commitment. QCOM -8.5% Tuesday. Bernstein (Market-Perform): integration risk flagged. Cantor raised PT to $200 (Neutral). Investor Day June 24 key catalyst. CEO Amon confirmed custom ASIC data-center chips pulled forward into CY2026.
articles.extracted_text: Qualcomm in advanced talks to acquire AI chip startup Modular Inc for ~$4B (Bloomberg). Deal announcement possible in near future. Follows advanced talks for Tenstorrent ($8-10B). Total potential AI silicon commitment: $14B+. Modular platform allows deploying AI models across different chips without rewriting code. QCOM -8.5% to ~$203 (Tuesday). Bernstein: 'strategic logic' but 'integration risk' — Market-Perform. Cantor Fitzgerald: PT raised to $200 (Neutral), bull case $300 on $30B DC revenues. Investor Day June 24. CEO Amon: custom ASIC DC chips pulled into CY2026.

articles.title: Broadcom Stock Drops After Strong Earnings — Time to Buy?
articles.summary: AVGO at $380, -3.1% on day, down 21% from $481 peak (June 2). Q2 revenue: record $22.2B (+48% YoY). AI chip revenue +143% to $10.8B. Q3 AI guidance: $16B (200% growth) — below $17B consensus. Gross margin guided to 74% (from 77%). Forward P/E now 32, PEG 0.68. Market cap $1.9T.
articles.extracted_text: AVGO: $380.14 (-3.06%), down 21% from $481 peak (June 2). Q2 revenue: record $22.2B (+48% YoY). AI chip revenue $10.8B (+143%). Q3 AI revenue guidance: $16B (+200%) — missed $17B consensus. Gross margin guidance: 74% vs 77% Q2 (product mix shift to lower-margin AI chips). Forward P/E dropped to 32 from 37. PEG: 0.68. Market cap: $1.9T. Three-year avg annual return: 67%.

articles.title: US Dollar Short-term Outlook: USD Breakout Targets Next Major Resistance
articles.summary: DXY at fresh 13-month highs. Technical: resistance at 101.77/92, support at 100.64/77. RSI overbought (first since March). Fed funds futures: ~70% probability of 25bp September hike. PCE Thursday is next catalyst. Key levels: close above 101.92 targets 102.72; below 100.64 threatens correction.
articles.extracted_text: DXY breakout to fresh 13-month highs. Resistance: 101.77/92 (September high-day close/high). Support: 100.64/77 (March high/1.618% extension). RSI overbought first time since March. Fed funds futures pricing ~70% probability of 25bp September hike. Thursday PCE report next major catalyst. 'A stronger-than-expected reading would likely reinforce the case for further policy tightening.' Bullish invalidation below 100.15/35. Close above 101.92 targets 102.72 and 102.95/99.

articles.title: Stock Market Live June 23, 2026: S&P 500 (SPY) Futures Under Significant Pressure
articles.summary: Pre-market: S&P -1.29%, Nasdaq 100 -3.17%, Nikkei -5.33%. Google down $6 pre-market after AI researchers departed. MU down $82 pre-market after +$77 Monday. BoFA raised MU PT to $1,500. Sticky inflation driving rate hike bets, rotation out of growth into defensives.
articles.extracted_text: Pre-market snapshot: S&P 500 at 7,398.30 (-1.29%), Dow 51,857.60 (-0.08%), Nasdaq 100 29,471.80 (-3.17%), Russell 2,983.20 (-0.75%), Nikkei -5.33%. SPY -$10. GOOGL -$6 pre-market (two AI researchers departed). MU down $82 after +$77 Monday (SK Hynix hit, hyperscaler AI spending concerns). BoFA reiterated Buy on MU, raised PT to $1,500 (from $950): 'robust demand and limited supplies through CY26-28.' Persistently elevated inflation driving traders to abandon rate-cut bets.

articles.title: US Stocks Fall as Global Tech Selloff Deepens
articles.summary: Morningstar US Market Index -1.3%. Mag7 YTD: NVDA +9.5%, META +12.0%, TSLA +11.3%, AAPL -9.9%, MSFT -23.7%, AMZN -14.4%, GOOGL +0.9%. SPCX opened -3%, recovered to ~$158. Saxo strategist: 'reset against a higher rate outlook rather than precursor to anything apocalyptic.' AI funding circularity concerns as firms tap debt/equity markets.
articles.extracted_text: Mag7 YTD returns (as of June 22): NVDA +9.5%, META +12.0%, TSLA +11.3%, AAPL -9.9%, MSFT -23.7%, AMZN -14.4%, GOOGL +0.9%. SPCX opened -3%, hovering above $150 first-day opening price (IPO at $135). 'There's a lot of froth in the markets. Sharp selling is likely profit taking' — Javier Correonero, Morningstar. 'Reset against a higher rate outlook rather than precursor to anything apocalyptic' — Neil Wilson, Saxo. 'Bulls pulling in their horns ahead of Micron earnings' — Wilson. AI debt/equity funding questions: firms tapping markets to fund capex.

articles.title: US 10 Year Treasury Note Yield — Eased to 4.50%
articles.summary: 10Y yield: 4.50% (-2bp from prior session). 30Y yield: 4.93% (-2bp). 10Y-2Y spread: +0.27 (positive, uninverted). 2Y at highest since February 2025. Mortgage rates 6.61% (30Y fixed).
articles.extracted_text: US 10-year yield: 4.50% on June 23, down 2bp from prior session. 30-year yield: 4.93%, down 2bp. 10Y-2Y spread: +0.27 (FRED, as of June 22). 2Y yield at highest since February 2025. 30-year fixed mortgage: 6.61% (WSJ). Previous close 10Y: 4.46% per YCharts.

articles.title: DXY exchange rate rose to 101.40, +0.37%
articles.summary: DXY: 101.40 (+0.37% vs prior session). +2.18% over past month. Fresh yearly highs. Support from hawkish Fed repricing.

articles.title: US High Yield Master II OAS at 2.65% — stable
articles.summary: HY OAS: 2.65% (vs 2.66% prior day, 3.13% last year). Credit markets stable despite equity sell-off — no dislocation in corporate credit. CDX HY 6M vol: 224.58 (+6.46%).
articles.extracted_text: US High Yield Master II OAS: 2.65% on June 23 (vs 2.66% prior day, 3.13% last year). Below long-term average, still near tights. CDX HY 6-month volatility index: 224.58 (+6.46%). IG spreads near 25-year tights at ~80bp (per InvestmentGrade.com, early April context). Credit not confirming equity stress — supportive for risk.
